title: Project Evidence Maintenance Next Execution Tranche
description: Select the single bounded maintenance tranche that best preserves reentry speed and lane honesty after the execution tranche lane exists.
question: Given the accepted sidecar lane stack and the current evidence-maintenance prioritization, which single maintenance execution tranche should Golem run next to preserve reentry speed and keep the project honest without reopening frozen fronts?

sources.label: oc_cli
sources.notes: Public reference surface still useful for grounding operator-facing maintenance work.

sources.label: oc_channels
sources.notes: Public routing surface that should remain contextual but not reopen runtime.

local_sources.label: local_current_state
local_sources.notes: Current state and accepted lane stack.

local_sources.label: local_execution_lane
local_sources.notes: New tranche lane contract and artifacts.

local_sources.label: local_prioritization_lane
local_sources.notes: Front-level prioritization contract that the tranche lane builds on.

local_sources.label: local_whatsapp_safety
local_sources.notes: Frozen safety frontier that must stay out of scope.

decision_criteria.label: Reentry Speed
decision_criteria.description: Favor tranches that reduce future retome friction quickly and materially.

decision_criteria.label: Truth Alignment
decision_criteria.description: Favor tranches that keep the accepted lane stack, frozen fronts and safety boundaries explicit.
decision_criteria.notes: A maintenance tranche should reduce drift, not add it.

decision_criteria.label: Maintenance Boundedness
decision_criteria.description: Favor tranches that stay compact and do not collapse into permanent PM overhead.

decision_criteria.label: Lane Guardrail Value
decision_criteria.description: Favor tranches that make it harder to accidentally re-open blocked surfaces or mix lanes.
decision_criteria.notes: Guardrails matter more than novelty here.

candidate_tranches.label: Truth Surface Refresh Pack
candidate_tranches.description: Refresh the truth docs, handoff and reentry path so the next operator can resume the project with less ambiguity.
candidate_tranches.goal: Keep the truth surface synchronized with the accepted lane stack and reduce reentry cost for the next concrete tranche.
candidate_tranches.relative_effort_note: Low because the work is local, bounded and directly aligned with versioned truth.
in_scope: Refresh CURRENT_STATE, handoff and runbook references after verified lane changes.
Keep one clean reentry path for the next operator.
Make frozen fronts and kill switches explicit.
out_of_scope: Adding new runtime features or reopening any blocked front.
Long-form roadmap writing disconnected from verify results.
WhatsApp changes or any live service work.
acceptance_criteria: Leaves one tighter truth surface than the current state.
Leaves one short retome path that matches the repo and the verifies.
Leaves frozen boundaries more explicit, not less.
preconditions: Only update docs after verified outputs or lane behavior changes.
Keep the work scoped to reentry and truth alignment.
risk_hints: Can become paperwork if it drifts away from the next real execution ticket.
Can over-update docs if every artifact is treated as canonical.
kill_criteria: Stop if the tranche stops reducing ambiguity for the next operator.
Stop if changes outgrow a bounded reentry package.
required_artifacts: Updated truth docs and handoff delta
One execution brief or ticket seed for future maintenance

implementation_ticket_seed.title: Refresh the truth surface and reentry pack
implementation_ticket_seed.summary: Tighten the versioned truth package and the retome path so the next implementation tranche starts with one crisp operational story.
verify: Manual reentry dry-run using the documented commands

candidate_tranches.notes: Most direct maintenance tranche for keeping the repo easy to resume.

candidate_tranches.label: Artifact Index and Retome Pack
candidate_tranches.description: Create a compact map from lane verifies and final artifacts to the commands needed to regenerate them.
candidate_tranches.goal: Reduce artifact drift and make it obvious which artifact matters when returning to the repo later.
candidate_tranches.relative_effort_note: Medium because artifact indexing can spread unless kept tightly bounded.
in_scope: Map final lane artifacts to their source commands and verifies.
Document a compact artifact-reading order for retomes.
out_of_scope: Building a full artifact warehouse or dashboard.
Treating every transient outbox file as long-term canon.
acceptance_criteria: Leaves one small artifact map that speeds up retomes.
Avoids turning artifact management into a new framework.
preconditions: Use only artifacts that already correspond to verified lanes.
Keep the map short enough to remain readable.
risk_hints: Can balloon into inventory work with little project return.
Can confuse canon with transient artifacts if not tightly bounded.
kill_criteria: Stop if the index gets longer than the reentry problem it solves.
Stop if the work starts requiring artifact retention policy changes.
required_artifacts: One small ticket seed or defer note

implementation_ticket_seed.title: Build the compact artifact index and retome pack
implementation_ticket_seed.summary: Map the lane outputs that matter to the commands that regenerate them so later reentry is faster and less ambiguous.
deliverables: Ticket seed or explicit defer note
verify: Manual spot-check of the indexed command list

candidate_tranches.notes: Useful runner-up if reentry speed becomes more painful than truth drift.

candidate_tranches.label: Lane Boundary Guardrail Refresh
candidate_tranches.description: Refresh the explicit boundaries between accepted sidecar lanes and frozen or dangerous fronts so future tranches cannot blur them easily.
candidate_tranches.goal: Make it harder for later work to quietly reopen blocked surfaces or cross safety boundaries by accident.
candidate_tranches.relative_effort_note: Medium because boundary work helps, but can also become meta-work if not tied to upcoming tranches.
in_scope: Make scope boundaries, kill criteria and frozen fronts more explicit across the upper lanes.
Clarify what the tranche lane must not reopen.
out_of_scope: General governance expansion unrelated to current lanes.
acceptance_criteria: Leaves clearer lane boundaries than before.
Makes at least one later misuse path harder to justify.
preconditions: Keep the work grounded in actual lanes and verifies.
Avoid adding new abstractions unless they close a specific guardrail gap.
risk_hints: Can add meta-language faster than it removes risk.
May overlap with truth refresh if not tightly bounded.
kill_criteria: Stop if the tranche starts duplicating existing lane docs verbatim.
Stop if the new wording does not close a concrete confusion path.
required_artifacts: One ticket seed or defer note
verify_requirements: Manual review that frozen fronts remain explicit

implementation_ticket_seed.title: Refresh lane boundaries and guardrails
implementation_ticket_seed.summary: Make the upper lane boundaries more explicit so future work cannot quietly reopen blocked fronts or blur scope.
deliverables: Ticket seed or explicit defer note

candidate_tranches.notes: Valuable if confusion rises, but less immediate than truth refresh.
